message:
refactor(signal): use libc constants instead of hardcoded signal numbers

Replaced all 10 hardcoded integer signal values in SignalKind::as_raw_value()
with their corresponding libc constants (libc::SIGINT, libc::SIGTERM,
libc::SIGHUP, libc::SIGQUIT, libc::SIGUSR1, libc::SIGUSR2, libc::SIGCHLD,
libc::SIGWINCH, libc::SIGPIPE, libc::SIGALRM). The previous values happened
to be correct for x86_64 Linux but signal numbers vary across architectures
(e.g. SIGUSR1 is 30 on macOS, 10 on Linux x86_64, 16 on MIPS).

Tests updated to compare against libc constants rather than magic numbers,
and extended to cover all 10 signal kinds (previously only tested 3).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
